solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public boolean canConstruct(String s, int k) {
        if (s.length() < k) return false;
        int[] count = new int[26];
        for (char c : s.toCharArray()) {
            count[c - 'a']++;
        }
        int oddCount = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < 26; i++) {
            if (count[i] % 2 != 0) {
                oddCount++;
            }
        }
        return oddCount <= k;
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution counts the frequency of each character and checks how many characters have an odd frequency. To form k palindromes, there can be at most k characters with odd frequencies (each can be the center of a palindrome). If the number of odd frequencies is less than or equal to k, it is possible to construct k palindromes.
